body_txt: Sometimes it makes for interesting drama to have The Rival come in and save the day . Being the Foil that they are, there's no way they can just up and admit to wanting to save their rival's life , now can they? So they need an excuse. After saving (or trying to save) their rival's life, they pass it off as having done it for their own reasons , such as one of these:  It's different from an Enemy Mine / Villainous Rescue situation because the two characters aren't really enemies... they just really don't like each other that much. The Aloof Ally is also a frequent user of this trope. If someone uses this trope with a stutter, that's a Tsundere with their Love Interest (who may or may not be their rival). Can also be said by a Nominal Hero . Sub-Trope of Think Nothing of It . Compare Ungrateful Bastard . Not to be confused with But for Me, It Was Tuesday .
